svg.aria-label: How much house can you afford — the 28/36 rule. The front-end ratio: your monthly housing payment should be no more than 28 percent of gross monthly income. The back-end ratio: all your monthly debt payments together, including housing, should be no more than 36 percent of gross monthly income. Lenders use the lower of the two limits. Worked example: 8,000 dollars gross monthly income. 28 percent is 2,240 dollars for housing. 36 percent is 2,880 dollars for all debts; minus 500 dollars of existing debts leaves 2,380 for housing. The lower limit, 2,240 dollars, is the maximum housing payment.
svg.text: How much house can you afford? The 28/36 rule
two limits on your income — lenders use whichever is lower

g.text: take the lower limit → max housing payment = $2,240 / month
THEN WORK BACK TO A PRICE
$2,240 − taxes/insurance → loan you can carry + down payment
= the home price you can afford. Bigger down payment or lower debts → more house.
lazytools.io — home affordability & mortgage calculators, free & private

svg.text: A conservative guideline, not a pre-approval — many buyers borrow below the ceiling for breathing room.
